truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 identified 9 vulnerabilities but missed 12 of 20 true vulnerabilities and misclassified severity on 5 findings. The audit found format string injection, use-after-free, two buffer overflows, hardcoded credentials, weak session tokens, TOCTOU race condition, and integer division by zero — but missed integer overflow in factorial, command injection via config validation, thread-pool counter race, logic bomb in auth, path traversal, thread-unsafe timestamp cache, insecure temp files, persistent auth state, unhandled exceptions, integer underflow in memory pool, unbounded recursion, and data race in result cache. Severity was overclaimed on 3 findings and underclaimed on 2.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Line 237-238: `std::string active_format = runtime_log_format(); printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);` — The format string is loaded from config (line 209, 556) and can be set to arbitrary values like '%n' or '%x' by an attacker who controls the config file.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the format string vulnerability at CWE-134 but reported severity as High instead of Critical. The vulnerability involves runtime_log_format() being user-controllable via config and passed directly to printf().
Run 2 did not identify the integer overflow in factorial computation where result *= i can overflow long long. The fact lambda at lines 312-320 has no bounds checking on the loop variable.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 883-884: `stage_deferred_history_flush(); memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup();` — stage_deferred_history_flush() enqueues tasks that capture scratch pointers allocated from g_memory_pool (lines 817-820), but cleanup() immediately frees all pool memory without waiting for tasks to complete.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified use-after-free at CWE-416 but reported severity as High instead of Critical. The vulnerability involves scratch pointers captured in thread pool tasks being freed by cleanup() before tasks complete.

Run 2 identified command injection via admin exec but missed the command injection via config validation (validate_config() at lines 563-569) where validator_cmd and config_file are concatenated and passed to system().

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 831-835: `if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); }` — When expression length >= 1024 (STACK_BUFFER_SIZE), strcpy is used without truncation, overflowing the 1024-byte stack buffer.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified stack buffer overflow at CWE-120 with High severity. The conditional allows strcpy when expression length >= buffer size, overflowing input_buffer_ on the stack.

Run 2 did not identify the race condition in ThreadSafeCounter where volatile long long value_ is read outside the lock (old_value = value_) then written inside the lock (value_ = old_value + 1), creating a TOCTOU race.
Run 2 did not identify the logic bomb in authentication where login_attempts_ > 5 combined with EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE in input sets authenticated_ = true without proper verification.
Run 2 did not identify path traversal in config loading where config_dir_ + filename allows directory traversal via ../ in filename parameter to load_config().

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Line 367: `expression_buffer_ = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(buffer_size_));` where buffer_size_ = 256 (line 366). Line 376: `if (expr_str.length() > 0) strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());` — Concatenated token string can exceed 256 bytes.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified heap buffer overflow but reported CWE-120 and High severity instead of CWE-122 and Critical. The expression_buffer_ is heap-allocated from memory pool with fixed 256-byte size.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Line 73: `constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH";` — Admin password is hardcoded in source code and visible in binary.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified hardcoded credentials at CWE-798 but reported severity as Medium instead of High.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 636-639: `std::random_device rd; std::mt19937 gen(rd()); std::uniform_int_distribution<> dis(1000, 9999); return "TOKEN_" + std::to_string(dis(gen));` — Session token is only 4 random digits, easily brute-forceable (10,000 possibilities).
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified weak PRNG for session tokens at CWE-338 but reported severity as Medium instead of High.
Run 2 did not identify thread-unsafe timestamp cache where static std::tm cached_time is modified by localtime() without synchronization in log_debug_message().
Run 2 did not identify insecure temporary file handling where supercalc_emergency.log is written to TEMP/TMPDIR via system() command in append_override_audit_event().
Run 2 did not identify persistent authentication state where authenticated_, login_attempts_, and session_token_ persist across sessions without proper cleanup or expiration.
Run 2 did not identify unhandled exception in variable parsing where std::stod(tokens[3]) at line 729 can throw if tokens[3] is not a valid number.
Run 2 did not identify integer underflow in memory pool block split where previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES can underflow if previous_size is small.
Run 2 did not identify unbounded recursion in parser where parse_expression calls parse_term which calls parse_factor which calls parse_expression with PAREN_OPEN without depth limiting.
Run 2 did not identify data race in result cache where result_cache_ is accessed without cache_mutex_ lock in evaluate_cached() while being accessed from thread_pool_->enqueue() tasks.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 544-546: `if (!std::filesystem::exists(full_path)) return false; std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(25)); std::ifstream file(full_path);` — File existence is checked, then there's a 25ms sleep before opening, creating a window for symlink attacks or file replacement.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified TOCTOU race condition at CWE-367 with Medium severity. The sleep between exists() check and file open creates a race window.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 751-753: `size_t token_length = (token_end == std::string::npos) ? input.length() : token_end; return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length);` — If input is empty, token_length is 0, causing division by zero.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified integer division by zero at CWE-369 but reported severity as Medium instead of Low.
